meta.title: Mercedes GLA200d GLA220d Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes‑Benz GLA200d / GLA220d engine — OM651 and OM654. X156 and H247 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

@graph.name: Mercedes‑Benz GLA200d / GLA220d Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes‑Benz GLA200d / GLA220d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned OM651 engine typically costs £1,500–£4,000 supply only; the OM654 costs £2,500–£6,500. Fitting adds £800–£1,400. Timing chain replacement on the OM651 can cost £1,500–£3,500 if caught early. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes‑Benz GLA200d / GLA220d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the generation. The OM651 (X156, 2013–2018) is rated ★★½☆☆ — it has timing chain stretch, injector failure, inlet manifold bolt shearing, and EGR cooler issues. The OM651 from 2012 onward (post‑balance shaft fix) is considered the value sweet spot. The OM654 (late X156, H247) is rated ★★★☆☆ — more modern but has valvetrain wear and AdBlue system faults.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes‑Benz GLA200d / GLA220d?

acceptedAnswer.text: OM651 timing chain stretch (the most critical — can destroy the engine), piezo injector failure, inlet manifold bolt shearing, turbocharger bearing collapse, and EGR cooler leaks. OM654 valvetrain wear (camshafts and tappets), cold‑start misfire, rocker arm bearing failure, and AdBlue system faults.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes‑Benz GLA200d / GLA220d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well‑kept H247 GLA220d worth £15,000–£25,000, a £3,000–£6,500 recon is a manageable 12–43% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a well‑maintained example. For an X156 GLA200d worth £5,000–£10,000, a £3,000 recon is more marginal.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes‑Benz GLA200d / GLA220d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The GLA200d and GLA220d use OM651 (X156 2013–2018) or OM654 (late X156 and H247). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the OM651's timing chain, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A timing chain and tensioner replacement (£1,500–£3,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£1,500–£4,000 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The chain sits on the transmission side of the engine, so it's an engine‑out job, but it's far cheaper than a new engine.

eraMap.title: The GLA200d / GLA220d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Early OM651 with piezo injectors and rear chain — timing chain stretch is the defining weakness.
Later OM651 — timing chain, injector and inlet manifold issues remain common.
Late X156 with the OM654 — modern 2.0 diesel; valvetrain wear and rocker arm bearing failure are the main watchpoints.
The current GLA — OM654 with AdBlue; AdBlue system faults and cold‑start misfire are the main watchpoints.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLA200d / GLA220d Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a GLA diesel engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Timing chain replacement on the OM651 is an engine‑out job, significantly increasing labour costs.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLA200d / GLA220d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: If caught early; engine‑out job but cheaper than replacement
Replace failed injectors with Delphi or Bosch
Replace manifold or fit repair kit
Catch it before oil system contamination
Cylinder head work required; catch it before camshaft failure
Replace NOx sensors, pump or injector; don't let countdown expire
Replace EGR cooler; check for coolant contamination

buyingChecks.title: Buying This GLA Diesel? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Mercedes service history with documented oil changes (more frequent than manufacturer recommendation)
* Post‑2018 OM654 — generally more refined than the OM651
* Evidence of timing chain work on OM651 cars
* No AdBlue system issues (H247) or evidence of NOx sensor replacement
avoidIf: * Cold‑start rattle with no timing chain history (OM651) — the single most important check
* Diesel smell from the engine bay — potential injector leak
* Cold‑start misfire or ticking (OM654) — potential valvetrain wear
* AdBlue countdown active (H247) — potential system failure
* Poor service history on any generation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The GLA200d / GLA220d is a stylish compact SUV that offers excellent fuel economy and refinement. But the engine choice determines your ownership experience. The OM651 (X156, 2013–2018) has well‑documented timing chain, injector, and inlet manifold issues — it's a robust engine when maintained, but neglected examples can become money pits. The OM654 (late X156, H247) is more modern but has valvetrain wear and AdBlue system faults. The OM651 from 2012 onward (post‑balance shaft fix) is considered the value sweet spot. Buy on documented history, and if you're looking at an OM651, demand chain‑history proof before you commit.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes‑Benz GLA200d / GLA220d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling noise on startup (OM651 timing chain warning) or misfire/judder (OM654 valvetrain wear)
* Service History — Including which engine era (OM651 for X156 2013–2018, OM654 for late X156 and H247) — see the Era Map above
* Diesel Smell — Any strong diesel smell from the engine bay? Injector leak‑off pipe or injector failure
* AdBlue System — Any AdBlue warnings? Countdown active? NOx sensor or pump failure (H247)
* Inlet Manifold — Any vacuum‑type noise under acceleration? Inlet manifold bolt shearing (OM651)
* Turbo Health — Any whining or loss of power? Turbo bearing collapse (OM651)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, injector, or emissions issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures; often point to EGR, DPF, or AdBlue problems

items.label: * Turbocharger
items.value: Single variable geometry turbo (VGT) or bi‑turbo

items.label: Most Common Failure
items.value: OM651 timing chain stretch and injector failure — approximately 55% of OM651 enquiries; OM654 valvetrain wear and AdBlue issues [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Peak Enquiry Season
items.value: Winter — cold starts expose timing chain and valvetrain issues [PATTERN DATA]
